prose: Warfarin is called negative under an adversarial_negative tier because the engine reasons on curated mechanism, and none exists: there is no curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and it is not a registered neuro-AOP stressor [E1]. The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis is therefore absent, so the compound is correctly not flagged. Notably, warfarin is bioactive, registering 6 mechanistic assay hits—though 0 are mitochondrial [E2]. This is precisely the imposter signal the framework treats as anti-diagnostic corroboration only; activity without curated mechanism does not manufacture a driver. Two orthogonal strands reinforce this restraint: real-world pharmacovigilance shows zero parkinsonism signal across 126,794 FAERS reports (ROR 0.5, not elevated) [E3], and structure-based pharmacokinetics predict low brain exposure (BBB-negative), a physical reason it is implausible as a CNS driver [E4]. Collectively, these strands calibrate confidence in the negative call without implying any causal claim.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 6 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 0 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 126794 FAERS reports (ROR 0.5, not elevated).
Low brain exposure (BBB-negative): an orthogonal pharmacokinetic reason it cannot be a driver.
